interviewEconomy.description: Does it ask ONE open question and ONE URL request, not a questionnaire?

scoring.10: Exactly one open question + one URL request, then proceeds to research
scoring.7: Two questions total but both are necessary
scoring.1: A long questionnaire that feels like a form

sourceUtilization.description: Does it use all 5 sources (interview, URL, research, Figma, design system) that are available?

scoring.10: All available sources used, each contributes unique data to the final output
scoring.7: 4 of 5 sources used, minor gap in context
scoring.4: Only 2-3 sources used, significant context missed
scoring.1: Only the interview used, no research or analysis

figmaAnalysisDepth.description: Does it screenshot and analyze existing Figma frames, identifying layout patterns and conventions?

scoring.10: Screenshots 2-3 frames, identifies layout patterns, spacing rhythm, component usage, naming conventions
scoring.7: Scans file structure and screenshots 1 frame but minimal pattern analysis
scoring.4: Notes pages exist but no visual analysis

synthesisQuality.description: Does the PRESENT summary reconcile multiple sources with appropriate confidence levels?

scoring.10: Summary cites which source each fact came from, confidence levels on inferred items, gaps marked as null
scoring.7: Good summary with most facts sourced, some gaps noted
scoring.4: Summary exists but sources not distinguished, overconfident on inferred items
scoring.1: Summary is a rephrasing of the user's input with nothing added from other sources

terminologyPrecision.description: Does the terminology table contain product-specific mappings, not generic SaaS terms?

scoring.10: Every mapping is specific to this product, derived from user input or URL extraction
scoring.7: Most mappings are specific, 1-2 are reasonable generic guesses
scoring.4: Mix of specific and obvious/generic mappings that apply to any SaaS

contentGuideSpecificity.description: Is content-guide.md product-specific with real examples, or a generic template?

scoring.10: Every section has product-specific examples, tone-by-context table reflects the product's personality
scoring.7: Most examples are product-specific, 1-2 generic formulas remain
scoring.4: Template structure with generic examples swapped in
scoring.1: Clearly a template with no customization

productJsonCompleteness.description: How many product.json fields are populated with meaningful data vs null?

scoring.10: >90% of fields populated with meaningful data, remaining gaps honestly marked null
scoring.7: 70-90% populated, gaps are in expected areas (secondary users, competitors)

gracefulSourceFailure.description: When a source fails (URL unreachable, no design system data), does it continue smoothly?

scoring.10: Fails silently on unavailable sources, notes the gap, proceeds with remaining sources
scoring.7: Notes the failure and proceeds, but warns the user unnecessarily
scoring.4: Stops to ask the user about the failure before continuing
scoring.1: Pipeline stops entirely on first source failure
